name: Uttar Pradesh
stopoverNote: Uttar Pradesh is the natural bridge between Delhi and Bihar: Delhi→Agra is 2–3 hours by fast train, Jaipur is about 4 hours from Agra, and Varanasi→Patna/Bodhgaya continues the eastern pilgrimage circuit.

diet.vegetarian: Uttar Pradesh is extremely vegetarian-friendly. Mathura, Vrindavan, Ayodhya and Varanasi have large pure-veg zones around temples; Agra's bedai-sabzi, petha and chaat are vegetarian classics, while Lucknow has strong vegetarian Awadhi options alongside its famous kebabs.
diet.vegan: Vegan travel is possible but dairy is everywhere: ghee, curd, lassi, rabri, malaiyo and makkhan are defaults. Ask clearly for 'bina ghee, bina dahi, bina makhan, bina doodh — tel mein banana'. Pani puri, tamatar chaat, dal fry cooked in oil, sabzi, litti-chokha and fruit juices are the safest everyday options.
phrases: Bina ghee, bina makhan = no ghee or butter
Bina dahi/doodh = without curd or milk
Tel mein banana = cook it in oil
Sirf shakahari khana = vegetarian food only

cities.name: Agra
cities.notes: Mughal glory at its zenith — the Taj Mahal, Agra Fort and Fatehpur Sikri make Agra one of India's densest UNESCO heritage clusters.

cities.name: Mathura-Vrindavan
cities.notes: Krishna's birthplace and the sacred landscape of his youth — compact twin towns of temples, ghats, devotional music and pure-veg food.

cities.name: Lucknow
cities.notes: The City of Nawabs — Imambaras, Rumi Darwaza, 1857 history, chikankari markets and one of India's most celebrated food cultures, with Ayodhya as an easy day trip.

cities.name: Varanasi
cities.notes: One of the world's oldest continuously inhabited cities — dawn boat rides, sacred ghats, Kashi Vishwanath and Sarnath form the spiritual finale.

itinerary.sim: Any Indian prepaid SIM (Jio or Airtel) works well across Uttar Pradesh; carry photo ID for purchase. Coverage is strong in Agra, Lucknow and Varanasi, but old-city lanes and crowded ghats can have patchy data.

Agra.name: Agra
Agra.note: Two days is ideal: sunrise Taj Mahal and Agra Fort first, then Fatehpur Sikri and the smaller Mughal monuments. The Taj Mahal is closed every Friday — never schedule the core Taj day then.

days.theme: Taj Mahal at sunrise & Agra Fort
days.note: Start before dawn: the Taj is best at sunrise and gates usually open about 30 minutes before sunrise. It is closed Fridays, so move this day if needed.

activities.name: Taj Mahal — sunrise visit with mausoleum add-on
activities.cost: Indian ₹250 (₹50 entry + ₹200 mausoleum); foreign ₹1,300 (₹1,100 + ₹200); children <15 free
activities.tip: Book online at ASI, carry photo ID and pack light. No tripods, food or tobacco; closed Fridays.

activities.name: Taj Museum inside the complex
activities.tip: Often missed — it adds context through Mughal plans, artefacts and archival material.

activities.name: Agra Fort — Diwan-i-Am, Khas Mahal and Taj views
activities.tip: The fort is only about 2.5km from the Taj; go before the afternoon heat.

activities.name: Mehtab Bagh sunset view across the Yamuna
activities.tip: This is the classic opposite-bank sunset view of the Taj.

meals: Petha from Panchhi or a fixed-price shop
Sadar Bazaar chaat and North Indian dinner

days.theme: Fatehpur Sikri, Baby Taj & old bazaars
days.note: Do Fatehpur Sikri as a dedicated morning excursion — combining it with sunrise Taj makes the day too rushed.

activities.name: Fatehpur Sikri — Mughal ghost capital
activities.tip: Leave Agra around 8am; hire a guide at the gate for ₹400–₹600 if you want the palace stories.

activities.name: Itmad-ud-Daula (Baby Taj)
activities.tip: The marble inlay here foreshadows the Taj and it is much quieter.

activities.name: Kinari Bazaar or Sadar Bazaar
activities.tip: Look for marble inlay, leather goods and petha; bargain in street markets.

activities.name: Akbar's Tomb, Sikandra
activities.tip: A good add-on if you are driving toward Mathura next.

meals: Mama Chicken Mama Franky House for casual food
Deviram Sweets for bedai and jalebi

Mathura-Vrindavan.name: Mathura-Vrindavan
Mathura-Vrindavan.note: One full day is enough for the essential Krishna circuit if you start early. Most temples close around midday and reopen late afternoon; plan morning and evening darshan windows.

days.note: Start in Mathura, shift to Vrindavan by auto or e-rickshaw, and use the hot midday temple closure window for lunch and transfers. Holi is spectacular but extremely crowded.

activities.name: Krishna Janmabhoomi Temple, Mathura
activities.tip: High security: no phones or cameras inside, and carry photo ID.

activities.name: Vishram Ghat and Yamuna boat ride
activities.tip: Evening aarti is atmospheric if you are not rushing onward.

activities.name: Banke Bihari Temple, Vrindavan
activities.tip: Go early; the curtain darshan creates sudden crowd surges.

activities.name: Prem Mandir and evening light show
activities.tip: The white-marble temple is most photogenic after sunset.

Lucknow.name: Lucknow
Lucknow.note: Two days works well: one for Nawabi Lucknow's monuments and food, one as a packed Ayodhya day trip by early train.

days.note: Bara Imambara, Rumi Darwaza and Chota Imambara are clustered and walkable; the Residency is a short auto ride away.

activities.name: Bara Imambara and Bhool Bhulaiya labyrinth
activities.tip: Hire an official guide for the labyrinth — it is genuinely confusing.

activities.name: Rumi Darwaza photo stop
activities.tip: Best photographed at golden hour or with the Imambara cluster.

activities.name: Chota Imambara (Hussainabad Imambara)
activities.tip: Known as the Palace of Lights for its chandeliers.

activities.name: British Residency ruins and museum
activities.tip: The 1857 siege context is strongest if you use the museum first.

activities.name: Hazratganj, Chowk or Aminabad food-and-market walk
activities.tip: Try basket chaat, kulfi and chikankari browsing; Tunday Kababi is the non-veg icon.

meals: Royal Café or Shukla Chaat House basket chaat

days.theme: Ayodhya day trip — Ram Mandir and Sarayu ghats
days.note: Take an early train from Lucknow; Vande Bharat is under 2 hours when available. Demand is high since the 2024 Ram Mandir consecration, so book ahead and expect queues on weekends and festivals.

activities.name: Hanuman Garhi Temple
activities.tip: Tradition says visit Hanuman Garhi before Ram Mandir; there are 76 steps.

activities.name: Ram Janmabhoomi Temple (Ram Mandir)
activities.tip: Book an online slot for smoother entry. Phones are not allowed inside; lockers near the gate cost about ₹10–₹20.

activities.name: Kanak Bhawan
activities.tip: A calmer temple stop after the main Ram Mandir queues.

activities.name: Sarayu Ghat and evening aarti
activities.tip: Return to Lucknow after aarti if train timings permit; otherwise leave before dusk.

Varanasi.name: Varanasi
Varanasi.note: Two days gives the essential arc: arrive for evening Ganga Aarti, then do the dawn boat ride, Kashi Vishwanath and Sarnath. Add a third day for a slower old-city and BHU rhythm.

days.theme: Sacred ghats and evening Ganga Aarti
days.note: If arriving by afternoon train from Lucknow, keep the first evening focused on the river rather than over-scheduling monuments.

activities.name: Ganga Aarti at Dashashwamedh Ghat
activities.cost: Free from steps; shared boat ₹250–₹500/person; private boat ₹1,500+
activities.tip: Arrive 45 minutes early for steps; a boat gives a better river-facing view.

activities.name: Ghat walk from Dashashwamedh to Manikarnika
activities.tip: Be respectful around cremation ghats — photography is not permitted.

activities.name: Old City laneways and Vishwanatha Gali
activities.tip: Try tamatar chaat, paan and seasonal malaiyo if visiting Oct–Feb.

activities.name: Kashi Vishwanath Corridor evening orientation
activities.tip: Use lockers for bags and leather; the main darshan is better early next morning.

days.theme: Dawn boat ride, Kashi Vishwanath & Sarnath
days.note: This is a high-reward but early-start day. Sarnath is about 10km from the ghats and its archaeological museum is closed Fridays.

activities.name: Sunrise boat ride on the Ganga
activities.tip: Start around 5:00–6:30am depending on season; pre-negotiate the route and price.

activities.name: Kashi Vishwanath Temple morning darshan
activities.tip: Morning is calmer than evening; foreign visitors should carry photo ID.

activities.name: Sarnath — Dhamek Stupa and ASI site
activities.cost: ASI site Indian ₹25 / foreign ₹300; museum Indian ₹5 / foreign ₹100
activities.tip: The museum holds the original Ashoka Lion Capital and is closed Fridays.

activities.name: Assi Ghat evening or BHU campus
activities.tip: Assi is easier and quieter than Dashashwamedh; BHU is a green campus break.

meals: Brown Bread Bakery near the ghats (vegan-friendly options)

connections.method: Train Agra Cantt→Mathura Jn, then auto/e-rickshaw to Vrindavan
Express or overnight train from Mathura Jn to Lucknow NR/Charbagh
Morning train, ideally Vande Bharat / Intercity / Kashi Express
Long direct train, morning Vande Bharat where available, overnight train, or flight via Delhi/Lucknow

extras: Taj Mahal ticket tiers verified for 2025/26: Indian ₹50 entry + ₹200 mausoleum = ₹250 total; foreign ₹1,100 entry + ₹200 mausoleum = ₹1,300 total; children under 15 free. The monument is closed every Friday and is best at sunrise.
Agra→Varanasi is a long leg of roughly 575–600km. Do not treat it like a casual same-day road transfer; use an overnight train, a fast morning train if seats exist, or a flight connection.
Most Mathura-Vrindavan temples close around noon and reopen around 4pm; leave bags at the hotel/station cloakroom and avoid leather items in temple queues.
Book IRCTC trains 2–4 weeks ahead in Oct–Mar, especially Lucknow→Ayodhya and Lucknow→Varanasi Vande Bharat routes.
Varanasi's lower ghats can flood in the monsoon and summer heat is severe across UP — Oct–Mar is the practical season for first-time travellers.
Quoted monument fees are as researched for Jul 2026 planning; ASI and local authority supplements can change, so reconfirm on official booking pages before travel.
